top_signals.title: Shell Sees 2026 LNG Trade Flat If Hormuz Reopens in Summer
top_signals.summary: Shell Plc expects global liquefied natural gas flows to be little changed this year — after more than a decade of growth — if shipping through the Strait of Hormuz returns to normal this summer.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: SEC wins $5.5 million default judgment over alleged fake crypto platform NanoBit
top_signals.why_it_matters: Potential liquidity or banking-system instability affecting financial conditions.

top_signals.title: Miners Turn South African Stocks From Leaders to Laggards
top_signals.summary: The precious-metals miners that propelled South African stocks to a record have become a millstone, dragging the benchmark index toward its worst quarterly slump in almost three years.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Retail Payments Infrastructure Board launches consultation on the next generation UK payments infrastructure
top_signals.summary: Today marks a major milestone in the modernisation of the UK's payments landscape, with the Retail Payments Infrastructure Board (RPIB) launching a consultation on the future design of the UK's next-generation retail payments infrastructure.

top_signals.title: Commission presents financial package for Montenegro's EU accession
top_signals.summary: European Commission Press release Brussels, 30 Jun 2026 Today, the European Commission adopted a financial package setting out the budgetary arrangements that would apply to Montenegro upon its accession to the European Union. Montenegro advances in the accession process and this proposal marks a significant step in the country's path towards becoming a member of the Union, following the latest agreement by the Member States to start drafting the Accession Treaty.
